summary: Hotz reflects on the impossibility of returning to simpler times when technical barriers created meaningful constraints and forced excellence. He argues that the internet, once mind-expanding, has become a set of narrow loops that are inescapable. The piece grapples with the realization that human obsolescence won't be dramatic resistance or acceptance, but rather humans becoming 'zoo animals oddly pacing' while the world moves on. He concludes that evolution—memetic and otherwise—continues regardless of anyone's predictions about history's end or AI singularity.
key_points: Technical barriers used to force quality and skill correlation—you can't recreate that with artificial barriers
The internet transformed from mind-expanding to constraining, and soon opting out won't even be possible
Choosing to do things the hard way doesn't make you an explorer—it comes from a different distribution than those who had no choice
Human obsolescence won't look like acceptance or resistance, but like zoo animals pacing in cages
Western institutions can no longer make sense of the world; we're subject to memetic evolution with no one driving
money_quotes: What if the Internet was a mistake? I feel like it's breaking my brain.
It was this mind expanding world in my childhood, but now it's a set of narrow loops that are harder and harder to get out of.
Once you have Starlink to your phone, not having the Internet with you will be a choice, not a real barrier. There's nowhere to hide.
But that didn't make him an explorer, it made him an idiot who died 5 miles from a bridge that would have saved his life.

It won't be humans accepting the rise of the machines, it won't be humans fighting the rise of the machines, it will be human shaped zoo animals oddly pacing back and forth in a corner of the cage while the world keeps turning around them.
Even if they hate you, it's more comforting to believe that they exist. That at least somebody is driving. But that's not true. It's just going.
The TikTok brainrot kids will be nothing compared to the ChatGPT brainrot kids.
To every fool preaching the end of history, evolution spits in your face.
key_insight: You can't recreate the conditions that made constraints meaningful—choosing limitation isn't the same as having no choice, and humanity's fate isn't dramatic obsolescence but becoming confused animals in a world that no longer needs our participation.
